title: jacobaea erucifolia
intro: Jacobaea erucifolia, also known as tansy ragwort, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, but has been naturalized in parts of North America. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to repel insects and its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Jacobaea erucifolia include reducing symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine.
